title: amaryllis × vittata
intro: Amaryllis × vittata, also known as the hybrid amaryllis, is a rare and exotic plant that is a cross between the species Hippeastrum vittatum and another unknown species within the Hippeastrum genus. It is known for its unique, white trumpet-shaped flowers with red stripes and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, contributing to its use in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. Amaryllis × vittata can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal tea or tincture, where it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its benefits and is said to offer relief from coughs, congestion, and skin irritations.
